column3: Description
Buttons allow the user to take actions or make choices.
Dropdown can open a menu or any other element when the button is clicked.
FAB (Floating Action Button) stays in the bottom corner of the screen and can reveal additional action buttons.
column5: +

column3: Modal is used to show a dialog or a box when you click a button.
Swap allows you to toggle the visibility of two elements using a checkbox or a class name.
A checked input with the theme-controller class applies the theme named by the input value.
Accordion is used for showing and hiding content, but only one item can stay open at a time.
Avatars are used to show a thumbnail representation of an individual or business in the interface.
Aura is a border light effect that can wrap around a component to highlight it.
Badges are used to inform the user of the status of specific data.
Cards are used to group and display content in a way that is easily readable.
Carousel shows images or content in a scrollable area.
Chat bubbles show one line of conversation and its related data, such as author, image, and time.
Collapse is used for showing and hiding content.
Countdown gives a transition effect when a number changes between 0 and 999.
Diff shows a side-by-side comparison of two items.
Hover 3D is a wrapper that tilts and rotates its content based on pointer position.
Hover Gallery reveals different images as the user moves horizontally over the image area.
Kbd is used to display keyboard shortcuts.
List is a vertical layout used to display information in rows.
Stat is used to show numbers and data in a block.
Status is a small icon used to show the current state of an element, such as online or error.
Table is used to show a list of data in table format.
Text Rotate shows up to six lines of text one at a time in an infinite loop animation.
Timeline shows a list of events in chronological order.
Breadcrumbs help users navigate through the website.
Dock, also known as bottom navigation or bottom bar, provides navigation options and sticks to the bottom of the screen.
Link adds the missing underline style to links.
Megamenu is a large horizontal menu whose items open popovers containing navigation links or custom content.
Menu is used to display a list of links vertically or horizontally.
Navbar is used to show a navigation bar at the top of the page.
Pagination is a group of buttons that lets users navigate between related content.
Steps show a list of steps in a process.
Tabs show a list of links or controls in a tabbed format.
column5: Tab 1Tab 2

column3: Alert informs users about important events.
Loading shows an animation to indicate that something is loading.
Progress bar shows the progress of a task or the passing of time.
Radial progress shows the progress of a task or the passing of time in a circular form.
Skeleton is used to show a loading state for a component.
Toast is a wrapper that stacks elements at a corner of the page.
Tooltip shows a message when hovering over an element.
Calendar includes styles for different calendar libraries.
Checkboxes are used to select or deselect a value.
Fieldset groups related form elements and includes a legend and labels.
File Input is an input field for uploading files.
Filter is a group of radio buttons in which choosing an option hides the others and exposes a reset control.
column5: <form class="filter"><input class="btn" type="reset" value="×"><input class="btn" type="radio" name="filter" aria-label="Option"></form>

column3: Label provides a name or title for an input field and can be placed before or after the field.
OTP is used to input verification codes, commonly for two-factor authentication or passwordless login.
Radio buttons allow the user to select one option from a set.
Range slider is used to select a value by sliding a handle.
Rating is a set of radio buttons that lets the user rate something.
Select is used to pick a value from a list of options.
Text Input is a simple input field.
Textarea allows users to enter text across multiple lines.
Toggle is a checkbox styled to look like a switch button.
Validator changes form-element colors to error or success based on native input validation rules.
column5: Enter a valid email

column3: Divider separates content vertically or horizontally.
Drawer is a grid layout that can show or hide a sidebar on the left or right side of the page.
Footer can contain a logo, copyright notice, and links to other pages.
Hero displays a large box or image with a title and description.
Indicator places an element on the corner of another element.
Join groups multiple items, handles first and last border radii, and supports horizontal or vertical layouts.
Mask crops an element's content to common shapes.
Stack visually places elements on top of each other.
Browser mockup shows a box that looks like a browser window.
Code mockup shows a block of code in a box that looks like a code editor.
Phone mockup shows a mockup of an iPhone.
Window mockup shows a box that looks like an operating-system window.

Semantic name / utility
primary
column4: Primary brand color; the main color of the brand.
column6: Use with theme-aware utilities such as bg-primary, text-primary, border-primary.

column3: primary-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the primary color.
column6: Use for readable content on primary surfaces, for example text-primary-content.

column3: secondary
column6: Use with bg-secondary, text-secondary, border-secondary, and component color modifiers.

column3: secondary-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the secondary color.
column6: Use for content on secondary surfaces, for example text-secondary-content.

column3: accent
column6: Use with bg-accent, text-accent, border-accent, and component color modifiers.

column3: accent-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the accent color.
column6: Use for content on accent surfaces, for example text-accent-content.

column3: neutral
column4: Neutral dark color for non-saturated interface areas.
column6: Use with bg-neutral, text-neutral, border-neutral, and neutral component variants.

column3: neutral-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the neutral color.
column6: Use for content on neutral surfaces, for example text-neutral-content.

column3: base-100
column4: Base page surface color used for blank backgrounds.
column6: Use as the primary page or card surface, for example bg-base-100.

column3: base-200
column4: A darker base shade used to create elevation.
column6: Use for raised or separated surfaces, for example bg-base-200.

column3: base-300
column4: An even darker base shade used to create elevation and borders.
column6: Use for stronger surface contrast or borders, for example bg-base-300 or border-base-300.

column3: base-content
column4: Foreground content color used on base surfaces.
column6: Use as the default theme-aware text color, for example text-base-content.

column3: info
column4: Color for informative or helpful messages.
column6: Use with bg-info, text-info, alert-info, and other info variants.

column3: info-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the info color.
column6: Use for content on info surfaces, for example text-info-content.

column3: success
column4: Color for successful or safe states.
column6: Use with bg-success, text-success, alert-success, and other success variants.

column3: success-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the success color.
column6: Use for content on success surfaces, for example text-success-content.

column3: warning
column4: Color for warning or caution states.
column6: Use with bg-warning, text-warning, alert-warning, and other warning variants.

column3: warning-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the warning color.
column6: Use for content on warning surfaces, for example text-warning-content.

column3: error
column4: Color for error, danger, or destructive states.
column6: Use with bg-error, text-error, alert-error, and other error variants.

column3: error-content
column4: Foreground content color used on the error color.
column6: Use for content on error surfaces, for example text-error-content.

column3: rounded-selector
column4: Border radius for small selector components such as checkbox, toggle, and badge.
column6: Consumed by selector components; available directly through rounded-selector.

column3: rounded-field
column4: Border radius for field components such as button, input, select, and tab.
column6: Consumed by field components; available directly through rounded-field.

column3: rounded-box
column4: Border radius for box components such as card, modal, and alert.
column6: Consumed by box components; available directly through rounded-box.

column3: selector base scale
column4: Base size scale for selector components such as checkbox, radio, toggle, and badge.
column6: Change globally or per theme to adjust the selector component size scale.

column3: field base scale
column4: Base size scale for field components such as input, button, select, and tab.
column6: Change globally or per theme to adjust the field component size scale.

column3: component border width
column4: Border width used by daisyUI components.
column6: Set globally or per theme to alter component border thickness.

column3: depth effect
column4: Binary switch that adds a depth effect to relevant components.
column6: Set to 1 to enable the theme's depth effect, or 0 to disable it.

column3: noise effect
column4: Binary switch that adds a background noise effect to relevant components.
column6: Set to 1 to enable the theme's noise effect, or 0 to disable it.
